records.ety: [It. carnevale, prob. for older carnelevale, prop., the putting away of meat; fr. L. caro, carnis, flesh + levare to take away, lift up, fr. levis light.]

defs: A festival celebrated with merriment and revelry in Roman Catholic countries during the week before Lent, esp. at Rome and Naples, during a few days (three to ten) before Lent, ending with Shrove Tuesday.
quotes: The carnival at Venice is everywhere talked of.

defs: Any merrymaking, feasting, or masquerading, especially when overstepping the bounds of decorum; a time of riotous excess.
quotes: He saw the lean dogs beneath the wall Hold o'er the dead their carnival
